calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-16-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-21-2017 Off Calendar

Defendant Charles Pankow Builder'S Ltd. Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Robert Stephen Arns, Jonathan E. Davis, and Shounak S. Dharap of the law offices of The Arns Law Firm PC (415.495.7800) appear as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, William A. Bogdan of the law offices of Lynch Gilardi & Grummer (415.397.2800) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Charles Pankow Builders, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Pankow has not satisfied its initial summary judgment burden. "It is well established that the pleadings determine the scope of relevant issues on a summary judgment motion." (Nieto v. Blue Shield of California Life & Health Ins. Co. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 60, 74.) Plaintiff Richard Johnson has alleged that Pankow breached a duty based on its right to control and its exercise of control over the details of work at the Panoramic construction project and that Pankow improperly stored potentially dangerous materials. (Complaint pars. 13 and 20.) The parties agree that Hooker v. Department of Transportation (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198 sets forth the applicable standard for when a general contractor is liable to the employees of its subcontractor under a theory of retained control. "[L]iability cannot be imposed on the general contractor based upon a mere failure to require the subcontractor to take safety precautions, where the general contractor's failure is not shown to have affirmatively contributed to the creation or persistence of the hazard causing the plaintiff's injuries." (Hooker, supra, 27 Cal.4th at p. 211, quoting Kinney v. CSB Construction, Inc. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 28, 30.) "[I]n order for a worker to recover on a retained control theory, the hirer must engage in some active participation. An affirmative contribution may take the form of directing the contractor about the manner or performance of the work, directing that the work be done by a particular mode, or actively participating in how the job is done." (Khosh v. Staples Construction Co., Inc. (2016) 4 CalApp.5th 712, 718 (citations omitted).) Pankow failed to show that it did not engage in active participation, i.e., that it did not prematurely order the doorframe alleged to have fallen on Mr. Johnson or direct where and how the doorframe was stored at the work site. Pankow merely relies on the terms of the subcontracts it had with Boyett Construction and Raymond-Northern California which state responsibility for safe storage and handling rests with the subcontractors. (UMF 12, 13.) This evidence is insufficient to show Pankow did not order and direct the placement and storage of the doorframe at issue in this case. Brannan v. Lathrop Construction Assoc., Inc. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1170 does not control the result here because Mr. Johnson's theory does not rest solely on Pankow's failure to remove the doorframe but also on Pankow's premature order of the doorframe and direction regarding where and how it was to be stored. The alleged violation of Cal-OSHA regulations does not create a triable issue per Seabright Ins. v. US Airways, Inc. (2011) 52 Cal.4th 590. Mr. Johnson's reliance on Evard v. Southern California Edison (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 137 is misplaced because the regulation at issue in Evard "required the owner of a billboard to maintain horizontal safety lines on the billboard [which] imposed an ongoing, nondelegable duty. The regulation in Evard imposed a permanent obligation on the owner with respect to the condition of the property; no one but the owner was in a position to ensure that condition." (Khosh, 4 CalApp.5th at p. 720 (citations omitted).) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Continued To Feb-21-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Robert S. Arns of The Arns Law Firm (415.495.7800) appears as counsel for plaintif/moving party, Richard Johnson

Attorney, William A. Bogdan of Lynch Gilardi & Grummer (415.397.2800) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Panko

Attorney, Paolo S. Kline Simon of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Schelley (925.734.0990) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing poarty, Raymond-Northern California, Inc.

Attorney, Nicole Jones of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415.362.2580) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Boyette Construction, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The cause is passed to allow counsel time to review the complaint. The cause is recalled and argued further. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF RICHARD JOHNSON'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff has not alleged sufficient facts showing that the conduct of the defendants, or any of the, was oppressive, fraudulent or malicious. While the better practice is not to deny a motion to amend based on the legal insufficiency of the allegations sought to be added by the amended pleading, the court does so here because: 1) that was the rationale for the August 24 order denying leave to amend and it would disserve judicial economy and result in inconsistent rulings to do otherwise; and 2) the parties have had a full opportunity to brief whether the new allegations are sufficient to support a request for punitive damages. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF RICHARD JOHNSON's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint is ordered OFF CALENDAR at the parties' request.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF RICHARD JOHNSON'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff Richard Johnson seeks leave to amend his complaint to plead punitive damages. However, the proposed amendment merely alleges that defendants "acted with the intent to cause injury to plaintiff or with a willful and conscious disregard to the rights or safety of Plaintiff." This does not suffice; plaintiff must allege specific facts showing that defendants' conduct was oppressive, fraudulent or malicious. (See Smith v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1033, 1041-42.) Prevailing party to prepare a [proposed] written order tracking the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
